sub_items.name: P95<200ms baseline test exists & meaningful
sub_items.notes: perf/api-load-test.js defines response_time: ['p(95)<200'] threshold with warmup/benchmark/stress scenarios and 7 endpoint test functions. Script is k6-runnable but not executed live.

sub_items.name: High concurrency throughput test (VUs >= 100)
sub_items.notes: stress scenario ramps to 200 then 400 VUs; benchmark scenario sustains 100 VUs.

sub_items.name: DB query perf analysis present
sub_items.notes: perf/db-perf-report.md (232 lines) exists with EXPLAIN-style analysis. However the dedicated perf-index migration (20260419000000_perf_indexes) is UNTRACKED on master.

sub_items.name: Redis cache strategy & verification
sub_items.notes: services/cache.ts is Redis-backed with cacheAside/writeThrough/readThrough strategies; 107 cache tests pass. BUT middleware/cache.ts uses an in-memory Map instead of Redis.

sub_items.name: API rate limiting pressure test
sub_items.notes: 18/18 rateLimiter unit tests pass. apps/server/perf/api-rate-limit-test.js k6 script exists. But rateLimiter.ts contains 6 NODE_ENV==='test' bypass branches that disable substantial behaviour during unit tests.

ac_results.summary: AC1 is largely scaffolded: k6 scripts and Redis cache service are in place and 125 unit tests pass. Significant gaps: response middleware cache is in-memory, /metrics endpoint exposes only memory/uptime (no p50/p95 percentiles), rateLimiter test bypasses are heavy, and live load-test results are absent.

sub_items.name: Slow query analysis & index optimization
sub_items.notes: schema.prisma has @@index lines and tracked migration 20260428000001_add_l2data_gin_index exists. However migration 20260419000000_perf_indexes is UNTRACKED (git ls-files --others).

sub_items.name: High-concurrency read/write test
sub_items.notes: apps/server/perf/db-performance-test.js exists with 100 VUs, 10m duration, p95/p99 thresholds for simple/complex/join/aggregation queries.

sub_items.name: Prisma connection pool configuration
sub_items.notes: db/client.ts declares CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE/CONNECTION_TIMEOUT/IDLE_TIMEOUT (lines 14-16) but they are NEVER USED — ESLint reports them as unused-vars errors. PrismaClient receives only DATABASE_URL verbatim. getConnectionStats() returns hardcoded zeros.

sub_items.name: Complex query (matching) optimization
sub_items.notes: optimizedMatchingService.ts uses Redis cache-aside (TTL 60-300s). But there is no queryAnalyzer.ts in apps/server/src/utils, no EXPLAIN-based runtime optimization.

sub_items.name: Sharding/sub-partition strategy evaluation
sub_items.notes: Grep across perf/, docs/, apps/server/ for shard|分表|分库 returns zero matches. No design doc or ADR evaluates sharding.

ac_results.summary: AC2 has strong index coverage in the base schema and a working db-performance k6 script, but the connection-pool config is a no-op, no sharding evaluation exists, and the dedicated perf-index migration tied to this issue was never committed to master.

test_results.lint_summary: ESLint on rateLimiter.ts/cache.ts/db/client.ts: 3 errors (CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE/CONNECTION_TIMEOUT/IDLE_TIMEOUT unused) + 1 warning (any in rateLimiter request type).

findings.description: Prisma connection pool configuration is dead code. CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE/CONNECTION_TIMEOUT/IDLE_TIMEOUT vars are read from env but never used; PrismaClient receives only DATABASE_URL verbatim. ESLint flags them as unused-vars errors.
findings.evidence: apps/server/src/db/client.ts:14-37; ESLint: 'CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE is assigned a value but never used'
findings.recommendation: Inject pool params into the URL via ?connection_limit=&pool_timeout= querystring (Prisma's documented mechanism), or remove the misleading constants.

findings.description: getConnectionStats() returns hardcoded zeros and is documented as 'placeholder for future implementation' — claim of pool monitoring is unsubstantiated.
findings.recommendation: Implement using pg_stat_activity SELECT or remove the function until functional.

findings.description: Performance index migration 20260419000000_perf_indexes (which adds composite indexes for ISSUE-INT002a) is UNTRACKED on master — created in working tree but never committed. The promised composite indexes therefore are not deployed.
findings.evidence: git ls-files apps/server/prisma/migrations/ does not list 20260419000000_perf_indexes; git ls-files --others does.
findings.recommendation: Either commit the migration with proper review, or remove it from the working tree if rejected.

findings.description: AC2.5 (sharding/sub-partition strategy evaluation) has no artifact. Repo grep for shard|分表|分库 in perf/ docs/ apps/server/ *.md returns no design notes.
findings.evidence: grep -rn 'shard|分表|分库' --include='*.md' returned only 评分表 (rating table heading).
findings.recommendation: Author an ADR or perf/sharding-evaluation.md describing the assessment outcome (even if conclusion is 'not needed for current scale').

findings.description: Cache middleware uses an in-memory Map instead of Redis. Multi-node deployments will have inconsistent caches.
findings.recommendation: Refactor cacheMiddleware to use the Redis-backed services/cache.ts get/set with namespaced keys.

findings.description: /metrics endpoint exposes only uptime/memory/pid; no p50/p95/p99 latency percentiles are emitted, making the AC1 P95 SLO unmeasurable from the running service.
findings.recommendation: Integrate prom-client histograms (http_request_duration_seconds) and expose Prometheus text format on /metrics, or include rolling p50/p95.

findings.description: rateLimiter.ts has 6 NODE_ENV==='test' bypass branches that short-circuit cleanup and disable limiters, weakening the 18/18 unit test signal.
findings.recommendation: Replace blanket NODE_ENV bypass with explicit injectable config and enable the limiter in dedicated stress tests.

findings.description: Performance middleware tests have 2/7 failing tests: 'should add performance headers to response' and 'should set X-Response-Time header'. Tests expect res.on('finish'), implementation uses res.end override.
findings.recommendation: Investigate why response headers are not being added — could indicate the perf header middleware is broken, which directly undermines AC1 latency tracking.

findings.description: matching service has no queryAnalyzer.ts. Optimization is limited to Redis cache-aside in optimizedMatchingService.ts. No EXPLAIN-driven analysis path.
findings.evidence: ls apps/server/src/utils/queryAnalyzer.ts -> No such file or directory; grep 'queryAnalyzer' apps/server/src/ -> 0 hits.
findings.recommendation: Either implement a queryAnalyzer that runs EXPLAIN on slow matching queries, or update AC2.4 acceptance text to reflect cache-only optimization.

notes: Server was not reachable on :3000 so /health, /ready, /metrics curls returned no body. Code review confirms /metrics has no percentile data. Backend-only issue — no UI to screenshot.
